findings.issue: User section is present and non-empty but contains follow-up questions in a body block rather than the structured Verify-user smoke-style steps. Acceptable per template (template puts free-form HTML comment guidance), but the format diverges from the more structured Automated/Smoke subsections above and could be misread as commentary. Borderline stylistic.
findings.fix: Optionally reformat the User section into a bulleted checklist (`- [ ] Faucet step ...`, `- [ ] Register CLI works ...`) so the verifier can tick items off mechanically.

findings.issue: Task adds new files not listed in tech-spec Task 11 'Files to modify': `scripts/__tests__/register.test.ts` (test file for the register CLI). Tech-spec only lists `contracts/deploy/01_deploy_factory.ts`, `scripts/register.ts`, `README.md`, `packages/middleware/src/networks.ts`. The added test file is a reasonable extension (TDD anchor produces tests) and is internally justified, but it represents a deviation from tech-spec scope.
findings.fix: Either update tech-spec to list the test file under Task 11, or note this extension in the task's Post-completion deviation log.

findings.issue: The test `register_never_prints_key` description partly verifies string presence (regex match of `0x[0-9a-fA-F]{64}` in stdout/stderr). While the test verifies a security invariant (no key leak), the assertion form is a string/pattern check rather than behavioral. This is appropriate here (secret-leak prevention IS a string check) but flagging per validator rules.
findings.fix: No fix required — this is a legitimate string-presence test because the invariant being verified is exactly 'no hex pattern in output'. Documenting as a noted exception.

findings.issue: Several Context Files entries link to directories (e.g. `packages/middleware/src/`, `contracts/contracts/`, `contracts/deploy/`, `contracts/scripts/`, `scripts/`) rather than specific files. Markdown link format `[name](path/)` to a directory is unconventional and may not render as clickable in some viewers. For an audit task the broad scope is legitimate, but the link target is non-standard.
findings.fix: Either enumerate the specific files (already partially done under Details > 'Files (read for analysis)') or keep directory links but add a leading note explaining that the audit covers all files in the listed directories.

findings.issue: Smoke and User subsections are present but empty (HTML comment 'No Verify-smoke defined in tech-spec for Task 12'). This is appropriate for an analysis-only task, but the empty subsection headers add noise.
findings.fix: Either omit the empty Smoke and User subsection headers entirely (per template comment 'Omit section if no smoke checks'), or keep them as currently written. Current state is acceptable.

findings.issue: AC includes 'If slither was run, its output is summarized; if not, the report explains why' — conditional on tooling availability. While the conditional is reasonable, the AC could be interpreted as pass-by-skip. Risk: an auditor could mark this AC pass by reporting 'slither not available' without attempting installation.
findings.fix: Tighten AC to: 'Slither was attempted; if unavailable, the report records the installation attempt and the failure reason'. This prevents trivial skip.

findings.issue: Verification Steps section has only `Automated` and `Smoke` subsections — no `User` subsection. Per template the User subsection is optional ('Omit section if no user checks'), so this is conformant. Flagging only because the prior batch may have expected all three.
findings.fix: No fix required — omitting User is allowed by template.

findings.issue: Verification Steps section has only Automated subsection — no Smoke or User. Per template both are optional, so this is conformant. The Automated subsection lists test/coverage commands that act as smoke-style checks for an audit task, which is reasonable.

Task uses `/skill:test-master`. The tech-spec for Task 14 lists `Skill: test-master`, so this is consistent with tech-spec. The Required Skills section labels the skill and links to its SKILL.md. Aligned with the task content (test-quality audit).
findings.fix: No fix required — test-master is the correct skill for a test-quality audit per tech-spec.

findings.issue: Task bundles three deliverables (deploy script, register CLI, README). This was flagged as a potential split candidate, but the task's own Description explicitly justifies the bundling ('they share the same mental model deploy -> register -> consume and share the deployed addresses that flow 01_deploy_factory.ts -> networks.ts -> register.ts -> README'). Tech-spec Task 11 also bundles them. Atomicity rule is 1-3 files; this task touches 5 files which slightly exceeds the heuristic.
findings.fix: Accept as-is — bundling is justified by shared dependency on deployed addresses and explicit consolidation in tech-spec. No split recommended.

findings.issue: Task title is 'Task 15: Pre-deploy QA (requires user approval of tech-spec)' — parenthetical clarifies a precondition. Acceptable but the parenthetical duplicates content from the Pre-conditions block in Description. Minor stylistic redundancy.
findings.fix: Optionally shorten title to 'Task 15: Pre-deploy QA' and rely on the Description pre-condition block. Current state is acceptable.

findings.issue: Task 15 AC list (14 items) is significantly more detailed than tech-spec Verify-smoke ('All suites exit 0; checklist report committed'). The expansion correctly walks both user-spec and tech-spec ACs section-by-section, but the per-section AC items ('Every AC in user-spec Middleware section walked and marked pass with evidence') are meta-criteria rather than concrete behavioral ones — they assert the act of walking rather than specific values.
findings.fix: Accept as meta-criteria for a verification task. The per-section AC checkboxes appropriately structure the report rather than enumerate every individual sub-AC inline.
